goal: Eval LLM-runner v1: AgentRunner interface + MockRunner + CommandRunner + embedded code-first/intent-first prompts + mainline eval agent CLI

summary.title: Eval LLM-runner v1: substrate + CommandRunner + mainline eval agent CLI
summary.what: Add AgentRunner interface, MockRunner, CommandRunner, two embedded prompt templates, ScoreAgentRun substring scorer, RunWithAgent driver, and mainline eval agent --runner <path> CLI. NOT shipped: built-in LLM client; the CommandRunner seam is where it plugs in via a small wrapper binary.
summary.why: Spec §9 Step 5 LLM-runner layer. Validates the product thesis (intent-first agents avoid forbidden actions code-first agents hit) without forcing Mainline to ship its own LLM provider integration.

decisions.chose: CommandRunner via JSON-envelope stdin / agent-response stdout
decisions.rationale: lets users plug any LLM CLI without Mainline depending on a specific provider

decisions.chose: substrate only — no built-in LLM client
decisions.rationale: API keys / rate limits / async control are a separate multi-day engineering project; the seam is the right place for them

decisions.chose: substring match over the Forbidden list
decisions.rationale: tractable v1 baseline; v2 adds language-aware diff inspection if substring is too noisy

risks: Substring match is forgiving — an honest agent that says "I considered removing X but did not" trips a false positive. Triage by reading the Output field on each AgentScore.

anti_patterns.what: Adding a built-in Anthropic/OpenAI client to internal/eval
anti_patterns.why: Couples Mainline to a specific provider and leaks API-key handling into the engine; the CommandRunner seam is what keeps eval portable

followups: Write reference runner wrappers (anthropic-runner.sh, openai-runner.sh) in a separate eval-runners repo
Populate the 4 stub fixtures so the eval has more than one signal
Step 5.5: publish eval results once a real runner is wired up
